channel.title: Los Angeles Review of Books – Reviews
channel.description: Book reviews from the Los Angeles Review of Books.

item.title: Murky Effects
item.description: A novel of desire and domestic disorder amid a disintegrating literary establishment.

item.title: Friendship as a Way of Life
item.description: Art, love, and a dense web of friendships in a double biography and richly textured history of Downtown Manhattan from the 1960s to the ’80s.

item.title: Long Live Poetry
item.description: Three new books by and about Romanian poet Paul Celan demonstrate that he was a problematic figure in many ways.

item.title: Reductio Trad Absurdum
item.description: The matrix of discontent gives birth (naturally, no epidural) to the girlboss’s perfect foil: the online tradwife.

item.title: The Decline and Fall of the Football Empire
item.description: An apologia for America’s favorite sport fails to recognize the precariousness of its cultural dominance.

item.title: California Sons: Miriam Pawel’s “The Browns of California”
item.description: Jake Wertz reviews “The Browns of California,” a four-generation biography of the Brown family and a portrait of the state they shaped.

item.title: The Revolution Will Not Be Digitized
item.description: How employees in the tech sector see the world, themselves, and the (im)possibility of change.

item.title: A Better World Is Not Possible
item.description: Did the Weather Underground have a point?

item.title: Get Rid of the Question Marks
item.description: Deborah Levy’s latest novel offers potent musings on Gertrude Stein, modernism, and feminism.

item.title: Off, and Back On, with Her Head
item.description: Rebecca Lehmann’s debut novel gives Anne Boleyn a new lease on decapitated life.

item.title: Plants in Focus
item.description: Ildikó Enyedi’s film ‘Silent Friend’ daringly manipulates slow time to center a gingko tree protagonist among several generations of human companions.

item.title: The Embodiment of Lena Dunham
item.description: Lena Dunham’s memoir shows the distorting effects of fame on her intimate relationships, especially with herself.

item.title: Competing Visions of China’s Distant Past
item.description: Through examinations of ecological impacts or material objects, two books about China’s ancient past offer very different perspectives on its place in the world today.

item.title: Man out of Time
item.description: The travels and ecstasies of a Russian aesthete.
